21.reason: [Loop increments i after reading a term, but the last valid index becomes i, not i-1. However, the multiplication loop uses i+1, which overcounts by one because i was not incremented for the last term due to break before i++. This causes reading uninitialized data.]

However, the multiplication loop uses i+1, which overcounts by one because i was not incremented for the last term due to break before i++. This causes reading uninitialized data.]

30.reason: [Same issue as above: j is not incremented for the last term, but multiplication loop uses j+1, leading to accessing uninitialized sn2[j] (where j is one beyond actual count).]

33.reason: [Should be k <= i or k < i+1 only if i was correctly counting total terms. But since i wasn't incremented after the last successful read, i is one less than the number of terms. So i+1 leads to accessing sn1[i] which was never assigned (only indices 0..i-1 and then the last read at index i without incrementing). Thus, this accesses garbage.]
